issue_title: Warm shutdown does not cancel prefetched tasks when using thread-pool concurrency
issue_body:
# Checklist - [x] I have verified that the issue exists against the `main` branch of Celery. - [ ] This has already been asked to the [discussions forum](https://github.com/celery/celery/discussions) first. - [x] I have read the relevant section in the [contribution guide](https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/main/contributing.html#other-bugs) on reporting bugs. - [x] I have checked the [issues list](https://github.com/celery/celery/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Issue+Type%3A+Bug+Report%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for similar or identical bug reports. - [x] I have checked the [pull requests list](https://github.com/celery/celery/pulls?q=is%3Apr+label%3A%22PR+Type%3A+Bugfix%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for existing proposed fixes. - [x] I have checked the [commit log](https://github.com/celery/celery/commits/main) to find out if the bug was already fixed in the main branch. - [x] I have included all related issues and possible duplicate issues in this issue (If there are none, check this box anyway). - [x] I have tried to reproduce the issue with [pytest-celery](https://docs.celeryq.dev/projects/pytest-celery/en/latest/userguide/celery-bug-report.html) and added the reproduction script below. ## Mandatory Debugging Information - [x] I have included the output of ``celery -A proj report`` in the issue. (if you are not able to do this, then at least specify the Celery version affected). - [x] I have verified that the issue exists against the `main` branch of Celery. - [x] I have included the contents of ``pip freeze`` in the issue. - [x] I have included all the versions of all the external dependencies required to reproduce this bug. ## Optional Debugging Information - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one Python version and/or implementation. - [x] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one message broker and/or result backend. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one version of the message broker and/or result backend. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one operating system. - [x] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one workers pool. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue with autoscaling, retries, ETA/Countdown & rate limits disabled. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue after downgrading and/or upgrading Celery and its dependencies. ## Related Issues and Possible Duplicates #### Related Issues - https://github.com/celery/celery/issues/4426 - https://github.com/celery/celery/issues/6932 - https://github.com/celery/celery/issues/3802 #### Possible Duplicates - None ## Environment & Settings 5.6.2 (recovery)

pip freeze Output:

aioquic==1.2.0 asgiref==3.11.1 attrs==25.3.0 black==25.1.0 celery==5.6.2 certifi==2025.7.14 cffi==1.17.1 cfgv==3.4.0 click==8.2.1 cryptography==45.0.5 curl_cffi==0.12.0 distlib==0.4.0 Django==6.0.3 filelock==3.18.0 identify==2.6.13 isort==6.0.1 mypy_extensions==1.1.0 nodeenv==1.9.1 packaging==25.0 pathspec==0.12.1 platformdirs==4.3.8 pre_commit==4.3.0 pyasn1==0.6.1 pyasn1_modules==0.4.2 pycparser==2.22 pylsqpack==0.3.22 pyOpenSSL==25.1.0 PyYAML==6.0.2 service-identity==24.2.0 sqlparse==0.5.5 Unidecode==1.4.0 virtualenv==20.33.1

## Minimally Reproducible Test Case

I have [a fork of the project](https://github.com/owenmjones/celery) which contains a working example as well as a suggested fix (though I have not yet written the unit/integration tests). The bug can be reproduced by checking out [this commit](https://github.com/owenmjones/celery/commit/8f600ea0d03addeb21f0bd7174e1d7aff6bff780) and executing the following steps: 1. In the `docker/` directory, run `docker-compose build` followed by `docker-compose up`. This will start the `app`, `celery`, `rabbit` and `redis` containers. 2. In the `app` container, run `./manage.py shell` and then run the following Python code: ```python from myproject.tasks import long_running_task for i in range(4): long_running_task.delay(i) ``` 2. While the first task is running inside `celery` container, invoke `docker-compose stop celery -t 180` to issue a `SIGTERM` to the main process in this container.

# Expected Behavior Celery should finish executing the running task and cancel all other tasks so that the container can finish shutting down and the broker can re-queue the tasks. This is what is observed when carrying out the same experiment in `prefork` mode, and is consistent with the [documentation](https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/main/userguide/workers.html#warm-shutdown): ``` [2026-03-06 03:19:06,254: INFO/MainProcess] celery@953467527ebe ready. [2026-03-06 03:19:44,187: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[b8731970-ecca-423f-9ea8-f587ee402c50] received [2026-03-06 03:19:44,188: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[7dc0b6f3-e518-4aeb-93c8-1f1ae7a1f44e] received [2026-03-06 03:19:44,188: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[ba7a115c-e040-44d8-ae2e-03fc14dda4d5] received [2026-03-06 03:19:44,189: WARNING/ForkPoolWorker-1] Started long-running task 0 [2026-03-06 03:19:44,190: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[bfcce87e-83ff-4c48-9799-a271639ec977] received worker: Warm shutdown (MainProcess) [2026-03-06 03:20:04,058: WARNING/ForkPoolWorker-1] Finished long-running task 0 [2026-03-06 03:20:04,059: INFO/ForkPoolWorker-1] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[b8731970-ecca-423f-9ea8-f587ee402c50] succeeded in 20.002562443999523s: None # ``` # Actual Behavior Celery finishes executing the running task, but instead of shutting down it then proceeds to run all other pending tasks. ``` [2026-03-06 11:11:01,891: INFO/MainProcess] celery@b7430a2faf6d ready. [2026-03-06 11:12:18,355: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[7f5ebd63-73d3-47e9-832f-2f5bc4dd576e] received [2026-03-06 11:12:18,358: WARNING/MainProcess] Started long-running task 0 [2026-03-06 11:12:18,359: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[a096fe18-b6b2-4972-b8d8-b2182c1fb94d] received [2026-03-06 11:12:18,359: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[9ad0fd6a-0b6d-465e-9620-b224d088f8d1] received [2026-03-06 11:12:18,360: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[78a06722-204a-468d-a110-3fe090c56f33] received worker: Warm shutdown (MainProcess) [2026-03-06 11:12:38,358: WARNING/MainProcess] Finished long-running task 0 [2026-03-06 11:12:38,407: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[7f5ebd63-73d3-47e9-832f-2f5bc4dd576e] succeeded in 20.049735991999114s: None [2026-03-06 11:12:38,408: WARNING/MainProcess] Started long-running task 1 [2026-03-06 11:12:59,329: WARNING/MainProcess] Finished long-running task 1 [2026-03-06 11:12:59,329: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[a096fe18-b6b2-4972-b8d8-b2182c1fb94d] succeeded in 21.00120838300063s: None [2026-03-06 11:12:59,330: WARNING/MainProcess] Started long-running task 2 [2026-03-06 11:13:21,246: WARNING/MainProcess] Finished long-running task 2 [2026-03-06 11:13:21,246: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[9ad0fd6a-0b6d-465e-9620-b224d088f8d1] succeeded in 22.00085314699936s: None [2026-03-06 11:13:21,247: WARNING/MainProcess] Started long-running task 3 [2026-03-06 11:13:44,249: WARNING/MainProcess] Finished long-running task 3 [2026-03-06 11:13:44,249: INFO/MainProcess] Task myproject.tasks.long_running_task[78a06722-204a-468d-a110-3fe090c56f33] succeeded in 23.00203331800003s: None # ``` This is because the `TaskPool` in `celery.concurrency.thread` calls `self.executor.shutdown()` with default arguments, and the `ThreadPoolExecutor.shutdown()` method has `cancel_futures=False` by default. If I modify the code to call `self.executor.shutdown(cancel_futures=True)` instead (see [this commit](https://github.com/owenmjones/celery/commit/e03bbb857bc2c834366bb634f201b9954e7828fd)), I observe that the pending tasks are indeed cancelled and are redelivered by the broker the next time the `celery` container is restarted (in line with the behaviour when using `prefork`).
